topic.what: Quantum-resistant quantum teleportation framework applying post-quantum cryptography to protect classical control bits
topic.how: Replaces vulnerable classical public-key crypto with PQC-protected channels while coupling quantum memory coherence to cryptographic latency
topic.why_matters: Addresses fundamental security asymmetry where classical correction channel is vulnerable to quantum adversaries using Shor's algorithm; classical attack surface must be secured for end-to-end quantum network protection

characteristics.what_it_measures: Maximum secure communication distance constrained by quantum coherence time
characteristics.evidence: Table I with Tcoh=1ms, fiber propagation
characteristics.context: Ranges from 191km (FrodoKEM-1344) to 199km (Kyber512) under 1ms coherence time

characteristics.what_it_measures: Required qubit storage lifetime in quantum memory
characteristics.evidence: State-of-the-art trapped-ion and rare-earth doped solid-state memories
characteristics.context: Practical coherence time consistent with current technology; determines maximum distance via linear relationship

characteristics.evidence: Estimated using JEDI BullSequana XH3000 supercomputer benchmark
characteristics.context: Kyber512: 2.3μs, Kyber768: 3.1μs, Kyber1024: 4.2μs, FrodoKEM-640: 15.7μs, FrodoKEM-976: 28.4μs, FrodoKEM-1344: 45.1μs

characteristics.what_it_measures: Joint classical-quantum attack success probability over time
characteristics.evidence: Mathematical derivation of Pjoint(t) coupling classical cryptanalysis growth with quantum decoherence decay
characteristics.context: Exhibits optimal attack window; decays exponentially after coherence time threshold

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fidelity of recovered quantum state under decoherence
characteristics.evidence: Phenomenological depolarizing parameterization modeling fidelity decay
characteristics.context: F(0)=1, F→1/2 as t→∞; reflects complete information loss to maximally mixed state

characteristics.what_it_measures: Information-theoretic upper bound on adversary information extraction
characteristics.evidence: Derived closed-form expressions for four stochastic leakage scenarios
characteristics.context: Quantifies residual information exposure under independent, sequential, burst, and correlated leakage models
